steps.title: Find support against replacement
steps.content: The researchers claim archaeologists are unlikely to be replaced even though the model categorized pottery as accurately as experts. The best support would show that archaeologists do valuable work beyond categorization.

steps.title: Distinguish help from replacement
steps.content: Choice A could make replacement seem more likely because the model is faster. Choice B says both sides made mistakes, which does not explain why archaeologists remain needed. Choice D concerns training, not irreplaceable work. Evidence that categorization limits time for human-only tasks supports the researchers' claim. The answer is C.
